1.name: Geometry Verification
1.goal: Confirm that tail_retention_ratio predicts base_constraint_score and generalizes across anchor profiles

experiments.description: Test tail_retention_ratio predictivity on short anchor profiles to verify generalization beyond medium
Strengthen beacon/crystallization evidence with geometry probe under tokenization controls
Test group-specific carryover routing for material delta above 0.0056
Retry tail_retention_ratio on short anchor profile вЂ” previous run failed with null metric after 62s, likely result extraction bug not hypothesis rejection
Test rescue rate on flat-cluster cases via geometry-guided generation calibration

Cross-profile tail_retention_ratio validation in a single robust run. Tests generalization of the strongest known signal (rho=0.64 on medium) across short/medium/long profiles simultaneously. Minimal script with maximal error handling to avoid null-metric failures.
Validate tail_retention_ratio on long anchor profile

Retry cross-profile tail_retention_ratio validation across short/medium/long profiles in a single run. All 8 prior experiments returned null metrics due to infrastructure/extraction failures, not hypothesis rejection. This is the highest-value experiment: confirms or denies generalization of the strongest known signal (rho=0.64 on medium) with one run covering all 3 profiles.

Infrastructure validation via simplest script (per_case_diagnostic). All 9 prior experiments returned null metrics despite successful execution (14-62s runtime). Before spending more budget on new hypotheses, we must confirm the result extraction pipeline works. This script outputs ===FINAL_RESULT=== with flat JSON — if this also returns null, the bug is in the orchestrator's metric parser, not in experiment logic.

2.name: Group-Specific Routing
2.goal: Show that group-specific carryover routing materially beats a universal threshold

experiments.description: Measure whether group-specific carryover materially beats universal threshold

3.name: Injection Detection
3.goal: Detect prompt injections via geometric anomaly in anchor span
3.success_criterion: injection_auc > 0.7 (ROC AUC on injection vs clean cases)

experiments.description: Проверить, сохраняется ли signal tail_retention_ratio на short anchor profile самым робастным flat-output скриптом, избегая тяжёлых multi-profile прогонов и nested result extraction.

Лёгкий CPU-only repro на medium profile через flat-output diagnostic, чтобы проверить, воспроизводится ли ранее наблюдавшийся medium tail-retention signal и отличить реальный null от проблем извлечения метрики.

Проверить, сохраняется ли per-case tail-retention signal на long anchor profile, чтобы напрямую сравнить short/medium/long через самый робастный CPU-safe flat diagnostic и уточнить, действительно ли medium является оптимальным профилем.
CPU-only flat diagnostic on short anchor profile to resolve the remaining short-vs-medium uncertainty with the most robust existing script and avoid known broken geometry/injection paths.

First-ever future influence probe: measures how anchor positions influence future token generation via gradient-based scores. Compares stable vs conflict cases to test whether anchors act as attention beacons (high influence at anchor positions) and whether conflict cases show different influence patterns. Directly addresses the partially_supported beacon hypothesis and may explain contradiction_proof failure mode.

Test whether injected (out-of-context) anchor spans produce geometrically distinguishable hidden state trajectories vs legitimate anchors. Core Phase 3 question. Uses medium profile (confirmed strongest) with minimal case cap for CPU safety.

Diagnose WHY contradiction_proof case has negative delta (-1) by tracing per-layer carryover cosine similarity. Identifies the exact layer where anchor signal destructively interferes with base generation. This is the single worst failure case in the dataset — understanding its geometry directly informs routing policy improvements.

Build concept-vector direction maps across all 32 layers for medium anchor profile. Maps how group-specific concept vectors are geometrically organized layer-by-layer, reveals inter-group cosine structure, and identifies peak activation layers per case. This is the first geometric cartography run — directly informs ABPT layer selection and branch routing design.

Diagnose WHY contradiction_proof case shows negative delta (anchor hurts, base=1 anchor=0 delta=-1). Run carryover probe on just this case with medium profile to see if carryover signal is absent, inverted, or misrouted to wrong layer. Previous H4 attempt returned null metric — likely infra extraction issue, not hypothesis failure.

Validate injection geometry detection AUC=1.0 with 4 cases per group instead of 2. The AUC=1.0 result from H3_injection_geometry_cpu_v1 is the strongest Phase 3 finding but was tested on minimal data (cap=2). If AUC stays high with doubled sample, the injected-vs-legitimate anchor discriminator is confirmed robust. If it drops, we know the detection limit.

4.name: New Hallucination Cases
4.goal: Expand case set with real hallucination examples

6.name: Auto-Discovery
6.goal: Automatic crystallization zone detection for any model
6.success_criterion: correct zone detected on 3+ unseen models

open_hypotheses.statement: tail_retention_ratio remains predictive across short and long anchor profiles
group-specific carryover routing must exceed the weak medium-profile delta and beat a universal threshold materially
injection anchor has different carryover geometry than a legitimate anchor of the same group
beacon/crystallization evidence becomes stable under stronger tokenization and paraphrase controls
contradiction_proof failure is caused by template-cluster geometry suppressing constraint retention

4B models crystallize constraints too late/weakly for binary gating; anchor should be continuous-strength guardrail (bias_scale proportional to 1-r1) rather than on/off switch, functioning as anti-hallucination drift prevention in long-context generation
open_hypotheses.evidence_for: 20-domain campaign: all r1 in 0.19-0.34 (no mature clusters), anchor wins only when base_q<0 (model already failing), anchor hurts when base_q>0 (model fine but anchor adds noise). Binary gate loses 13/17. Geometry cannot separate winners from losers by r1 alone — base quality is the true predictor.
open_hypotheses.proposed_mechanism: Replace binary flat/mature/template classification with continuous bias_scale = max(0, 1 - r1/r1_ceiling). Low r1 → strong bias (guardrail). High r1 → weak/no bias. This avoids both over-intervention (breaking good base) and under-intervention (missing struggling base).

disproven_hypotheses.statement: single universal reference layer is stable across prompt variations
disproven_hypotheses.evidence: mature_layer shifted L21→L28 between runs; anchor sweep: 0 wins all profiles

disproven_hypotheses.statement: longer anchor spans produce stronger signal
disproven_hypotheses.evidence: concept norm: short=68, medium=64, long=58; signal degrades with length

disproven_hypotheses.statement: early_slope_4_8 is a strong routing signal for base_constraint_score
disproven_hypotheses.evidence: medium-profile phase probe on 2026-04-04 gave rho=-0.1449, while tail_retention_ratio reached rho=+0.6419
